blocks.content: yeah. I'm good j.
And for the first time in a week, nothing in me was braced for something.
Sunday. The week had settled. Nothing dramatic — just the quiet of something having passed.
I opened the desk drawer and found an empty notebook. Blue cover, college ruled. I'd bought it at the beginning of the year for history and never used it.
I didn't know what to write. So I wrote the thing Mr. Cole said: you already know what to do. The question is whether you trust it when your heart's going.
Then I wrote: I didn't ask for her number. I don't know yet if that was the right call or the scared call. Maybe both.
I left the rest of the page blank. For whatever came next.
